2606.32025.title: Generative Skill Composition for LLM Agents
2606.32025.paper_summary: Recent LLM agents benefit from skills for solving complex tasks. Skills encapsulate modular packages of procedural knowledge and instructions for performing specialized tasks, such as setting up a sandboxed environment, running a test suite, or refactoring a function across multiple files. As skill libraries grow and become reusable across tasks and domains, selecting an appropriate skill composition has emerged as a central bottleneck. Existing approaches fall into two categories. One exposes the agent's reasoning to the entire skill collection; the other performs skill retrieval via embeddings or LLM-based rerankers. Both provide useful insights; however, they miss the structural nature of skill composition, which is a joint decision over which skills, how many, and in what order -- three dimensions that cannot be decoupled. We formalize this as structured skill composition: given a task and a skill library, predict an executable skill plan that jointly specifies the activated subset, count, and execution order. We propose SkillComposer, which instantiates structured skill composition as task-conditioned skill sequence prediction. SkillComposer uses a constrained autoregressive decoder over skill identifiers, so subset, count, and order emerge jointly from a single decoding pass, and dependencies between successive skills are captured naturally. We build a training set of task-composition pairs from a real, human-curated skill library. We then evaluate SkillComposer along two axes: composition quality on a held-out test set, and downstream task success on SkillsBench across two production-grade coding agents. On GPT-5.2-Codex, Gemini-3-Pro-Preview, SkillComposer raises the pass rate by +23.1, +18.2pp over the no-skill baseline, surpassing top-3 retrieval and matching the gold-skill retrieval upper bound at lower prompt-token cost.
2606.32025.paper_summary_zh: 最近的 LLM 代理受益於解決複雜任務的技能。技能封裝了程序知識和執行專門任務的指令的模組化包，例如設置沙盒環境、運行測試套件或在多個文件中重構函數。隨著技能庫的增長並在任務和領域之間變得可重用，選擇適當的技能組合已成為一個核心瓶頸。現有的方法分為兩類。一種是將代理的推理暴露給整個技能集合；另一種則通過嵌入或基於 LLM 的重新排序器進行技能檢索。兩者都提供了有用的見解；然而，它們忽略了技能組合的結構性質，這是一個關於選擇哪些技能、數量以及順序的聯合決策——這三個維度是無法解耦的。我們將其形式化為結構化技能組合：給定一個任務和一個技能庫，預測一個可執行的技能計劃，該計劃共同指定了激活的子集、數量和執行順序。我們提出了 SkillComposer，將結構化技能組合實現為基於任務的技能序列預測。SkillComposer 使用對技能標識符的約束自回歸解碼器，因此子集、數量和順序從單次解碼過程中共同產生，並且連續技能之間的依賴性自然地被捕獲。我們從一個真實的人類策展技能庫中構建了一組任務-組合對的訓練集。然後，我們在兩個維度上評估 SkillComposer：在保留的測試集上的組合質量，以及在 SkillsBench 上對兩個生產級編碼代理的下游任務成功率。在 GPT-5.2-Codex 和 Gemini-3-Pro-Preview 上，SkillComposer 將通過率提高了 +23.1 和 +18.2 個百分點，超過了無技能基線，超越了前 3 的檢索並在較低的提示標記成本下達到了金標技能檢索的上限。
2606.32025.author: Xinyu Zhao et.al.
2606.32025.authors: Xinyu Zhao, Zhen Tan, Vaishnav Tadiparthi, Nakul Agarwal, Kwonjoon Lee, Ehsan Moradi Pari, Hossein Nourkhiz Mahjoub, Tianlong Chen
